item.title: DirectAccess with ECDSA certificates
item.description: By default when configuring DirectAccess it will only accept client certificates that have an RSA key. > Import-Module -Force .\directaccess.psm1 > Set-NetIPsecPhase1AuthSetAlgorithms -Authority "C=US, O=DigiCert Inc, OU=www.digicert.com, CN=DigiCert Assured ID Root CA" -Algorithm "RSA","ECDSA256" function Set-NetIPsecPhase1AuthSetAlgorithms { [CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess)] param( [System.String] $Domain = $(Get-ADDomain).DNSRoot, [System.String] $GPO = [string] 'DirectAccess Server Settings', [System.String] $RuleName = 'DirectAccess Policy-DaServerToCorp', [Parameter(mandatory = $true)] [System.String] $Authority, [ValidateSet("ECDSA256", "ECDSA384", "RSA")] [System.String[]] $Algorithm = @("RSA"), [Parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [switch] $Force ) try { #Sanity check that the GPO exists.
